intro.title: What is Java?
intro.description: Java is a class-based, object-oriented programming language designed to have minimal implementation dependencies. It’s widely used across enterprise, mobile application developement, and web platforms.
points: Platform independent via JVM.
Object-Oriented and secure.
Multithreading and garbage collection support.
Popular in enterprise, Android developement, and web development.

intro.title: JDK vs JRE vs JVM
points: JDK: The Java Development Kit (JDK) is a software development kit that provides a set of tools and libraries for developing Java applications. It includes the Java Runtime Environment (JRE), which is necessary for running Java programs, as well as development tools such as compilers and debuggers. The JDK is essential for developers who want to write, compile, and run Java code.

JRE:The Java Runtime Environment (JRE) is a package that provides the libraries and JVM required to run Java applications. It does not include development tools, making it suitable for users who only need to run Java programs without developing them. The JRE is a subset of the JDK and is included in the JDK installation.

JVM: The Java Virtual Machine (JVM) is a crucial component of the JRE that provides the runtime environment for Java programs. It is responsible for executing Java bytecode on any platform that supports JVM, making Java a “write once, run anywhere” language. The JVM is included in the JRE and is not installed separately.

intro.title: Features of Java
points: Simple : Easy to learn ,with syntax similar to c++ but without complex features.
Object-Oriented : Everything is treated as object
Platform-independent : Java code rund on any machine with a java virtual machile
Robust : Strong memory management, exception handling,and type checking
Multithreaded : Supports multiple programming
Architectural Neural : No dependency on processor architecture
Portable : Java bytecode can be transferred and run on any platform
High Performance : Just-In-Time(JIT) compiler improves performance.
Secure : Provides a secure environment with features like bytecode verification
sandboxing : Sandboxing is a security mechanism in Java that restricts the execution of untrusted code in a controlled environment
Distributed : Designed for nuilding distributed application
Dynamic : Supports dynamic linking of classes and methods .

intro.title: Disadvantages of Java
points: Slower Performance than Native Languages(like c or c++)
Memory consumption (Automatic garbage Collection and heavy JVM and not ideal for systems like low RAM)
Verbose Syntax (Java code can be very long-winded compared to modern languaes like python and kotlin .)
Poor GUI Support (Java's GUI liberaries (like Swing and AWT)(Outdated) (Incosstent in look across platforms) JavaFX is better ,but still not widely used for modern UIs.
No Control Over Low-Level Aspects (Java abstracts away memory management and hardware access)(not suitable for system level programming like device drivers or OS kernals)
Security Issues in Order Java Versions (Java Applets and Outdates plugins were once majore security risks through now deprecated,they left a legacy of trust issues .
Hight Startup Time (Java application take longer to start compared to native or scripting languages , this is due to jvm startup and class loading )
Backward Compatibility Bloat (Java is very backward compatible , But this leads to bloat and legacy code that must be supported in JVM)

install.title: Download and Install JDK
points: Go to the official Oracle website or OpenJDK provider.
Download the latest version of JDK suitable for your OS.
Run the installer and follow installation steps.
Verify installation using: java -version in terminal.

install.title: Setting Up Environment Variables
points: Open System Properties → Advanced → Environment Variables.
Create a new system variable named JAVA_HOME.
Set JAVA_HOME to your JDK installation path.
Add %JAVA_HOME%\bin to the Path variable.
Verify using: javac -version in terminal.

install.title: Writing and Running First Java Program
points: Create a file named HelloWorld.java.
Write a simple main method.
Compile using: javac HelloWorld.java.
Run using: java HelloWorld.

install.title: Using IDEs (Eclipse, IntelliJ IDEA, VS Code)
points: Download and install your preferred IDE.
Create a new Java project.
Write and run Java programs easily with built-in tools.
Use debugger and extensions for better development.

identifier.title: Definition of Identifiers
identifier.description: An identifier is the name used to identify classes, methods, variables, labels, and other user-defined elements in Java. In simple terms, identifiers are the names you give to elements in your program.

identifier.title: Rules for Naming Identifiers
identifier.description: Java has strict rules for naming identifiers. If you break any rule, the compiler will throw an error.
points: ✔️ Can contain letters (A-Z, a-z), digits (0-9), underscores (_), and dollar signs ($).
❌ Cannot start with a digit.
❌ Cannot use Java reserved keywords (e.g., int, class, public).
✔️ Can be of any length.
✔️ Java is case-sensitive (name and Name are different).
✔️ Unicode characters are allowed (avoid unless necessary).
❌ No spaces allowed in identifiers.

identifier.title: Valid Identifier Examples
Invalid Identifier Examples
identifier.code:
int 1stNumber; // Starts with a digit
String full name; // Contains space
float class; // 'class' is a reserved keyword

identifier.title: Java Naming Conventions (CamelCase, PascalCase)
identifier.description: Java developers follow standard naming conventions for readability and consistency.

table.description: First letter of each word capitalized

DataType.title: Definition of Data Types
points: Every data has some type, and that type is known as a Data Type.
Data type defines which type of data a variable can hold or store in memory.

DataType.title: Types of Data Types
points: 1. Primitive Data Type
2. Non-Primitive Data Type

DataType.title: Primitive Data Types
DataType.description: These are the data types that will store only values. To use these primitive data types, we have to follow certain rules.
points: If we want to use a long value in our program, we will use L/l at the end of the value as a suffix.
To differentiate in between float and double value, we will use F/f for float and D/d for double value as a suffix.
Using D/d is not mandatory; decimal values are double by default.
Char values use single quotes and contain only one character.
Boolean values must always be lowercase.

DataType.title: Non-Primitive Data Types
DataType.description: These are the data types that will store the objects only.Non-primitive data types (also called reference types) are types that refer to objects and do not store the actual data directly. They are created by the programmer (except for built-in ones like String, Array, etc.).Unlike primitive types (like int, char, etc.), non-primitive types store memory addresses (references) of objects.

DataType.title: Example Program

typecasting.title: TYPE CASTING
points: The process of converting one data type value into another data type is known as Typecasting.
It is mostly applied to primitive data types (except boolean).
This process is also called Primitive Data Type Casting.
There are 2 types of Typecasting in Java:
a. Implicit Type Casting
b. Explicit Type Casting

typecasting.title: IMPLICIT TYPE CASTING
points: Also known as: Widening or Upcasting
When a smaller data type is converted into a bigger data type automatically by the compiler.
There is no loss of data.
Happens in this order:
byte → short → int → long → float → double

typecasting.title: Explicit Type Casting
points: Also known as: Narrowing or Downcasting
When a bigger data type is converted into a smaller data type manually by the programmer.
There may be loss of data (especially fractional or overflow values).
This typecasting requires the use of type in brackets.

typecasting.title: Example Programs

variables.title: What is a Variable?
variables.description: Variable is a name or reference to a memory allocation where we are going to store our data.Data can be values as well as objects.
points: There are 2 parts of variable.
1. Declaration
2. Initialisation
Declaration is mandatory whereas initialization is optional.
If we are not initializing, JVM will provide default value according to the data type.

variables.title: Types of Variables
variables.description: categorization of the variable has been done on the basis of where we are declaring the variable and how we are declaring the variable.
points: 1. Non-Static variable
2. Static variable
3. Local Variable

variables.title: Non-Static Variable
variables.description: These are the variables whose values differs from object to object. Every object will have a separate copy of non-static variable.
points: The non-static variables are declared inside the class but outside the method block or constructor.
There is only one way in which we can access a non-static variable and that is by the help of object reference.
The non-static variables are stored in Heap area.
The memory of the non-static variable will be allocated after the creation of the object and that to be inside of the object.
Two non-static variable names cannot be same.

variables.title: Static Variable
variables.description: These variables have the same value for all objects of the class. Only one copy of a static variable is created and shared among all objects. Static variables are also called Class Variables.
points: Static variables are declared inside the class but with the static keyword, and outside of method blocks or constructors.
There are two ways to access a static variable:
1.	Using class name (Recommended)
2.	Using object reference
Static variables are stored in the Method Area (also called Class Area).
Memory for static variables is allocated only once when the class is loaded by the JVM.

variables.title: Local Variable
variables.description: A local variable is declared inside a method, constructor, or block. It is created when the method is called and destroyed when the method finishes. Local variables are not accessible outside the method.
points: Local variables must be initialized before use.
They are stored in the Stack area.
No default values are given by JVM to local variables.

operators.title: Operator Definition & Types
operators.description: Operators are the special symbols using which we can perform certain operations on operands.There are 3 types of operator present in java and these categorization has been done on the basis of how many operands we are using.
points: Unary Operator
Binary Operator
Ternary Operator

operators.title: Unary Operators
operators.description: Whenever we are performing any operation using only one operand, it is known as Unary Operator.
points: Increment Operator (++): It is a type of operator when used, will always increase the value of the variable by adding 1 to it. There are 2 types of increment operation.
1.Pre-Increment (++x) : In pre-increment, first the value will be increased then after it will get assigned.
2.Post-Increment (x++) : In post increment first the value will be assigned then after it will get incremented.
Decrement Operator (--) : It is a type of operator when used will always decrease the value of the variable by substracting 1 to it. There are 2 types of Decrement operation.
1.Pre-Decrement Operation(--x) : Whenever we are performing pre-decrement operation then it will first decrease the value then after it will assign.
2.Post-Decrement Operation(x--) : Whenever we are performing post-decrement operation then it will first assign the value thenafter it will get decremented.
Bitwise Complement (~) : In bitwise compliment it will first add 1 to the value then it will change the symbol of the final output.
Boolean Complement (!) : In Boolean compliment it will just change the Boolean value from true to false and false to true.

operators.title: Binary Operators
operators.description: Binary operators are the operators that will be used to perform operation with 2 operands.
points: Arithmetic Operators : These are the operators that will perform arithmetic operations like addition, subtraction,  etc.  They are:-	'+' :-add 	'-' :-subtract	 '*' :-multiply 	'/' :-divide '%' :-modulo

When we are using mod or divide operator both will perform division operation but the outputwill be different because mod will always take remainder as the output and divide will take quotient as the output.
String Concatenation : It is a process of merging two operand where both the operands can be string or any one of the the operand must be a string value.

-->In order to perform string concatenation we will be using add/plus (+) operator and it is also known as Overloaded operator because it can perform both addition and concatenation  simultaneously in the same line.

Assignment Operator : It is a type of operator which will assign value to the variable and variable to the object. For primitive it will be value to variable *(right to left) and for non-primitive it will be variable to object  *(left to right).
Relational Operators : These are the operators which will be used to check the relationship between 2 operands like '>' :-greater than '<' :-lesser than
Some compound relational operator are:-	'>=' :-greater than equal to '<=' :-lesser than equal to
The output of the Relational operator will always be in the form of Boolean expression.

Equality Operators : It is a type of operator which will be checking whether both the operand are equal or not. If it is same then the output will be true otherwise false. Equality operator's output will also be in the boolean expression. It is applicable for both primitive as well as non primitive data type.

Not equal Operators : It is type of operator which will be checking whether the operand are not equal or same. If not equal then it will give true as the output other wise false. Not equality operator's output will also be in the boolean expression. It is applicable for both primitive as well as non primitive data type.
Bitwise Operators : There are 3 types of BITWISE operator. 1.Bitwise AND (&) 2.Bitwise OR (|)  3.Bitwise XOR (^)
1.Bitwise AND (&) : In bitwise AND operator it will give us true as the output if both the operands output is true otherwise false.
2.Bitwise OR (|) : In bitwise OR operator it will give us true if any one of the operands output is true otherwise false.
3.Bitwise XOR (^) : In bitwise XOR operator it will give as true if both the operands output are different.
Logical Operators : In logical operator the execution of the 2nd operand is based on the output of the first operand. There are 2 types of Logical Operator:- 1.Logical AND (&&)  2.Logical OR (||)
1.Logical AND (&&) : In logical AND operator the 2nd operand will be executed if and only if(iff) the output of the first operand is true.
2.Logical OR (||) : In logic OR operator the 2nd operand will only be executed iff the output of the first operand is false.

operators.title: Ternary Operator
operators.description: Whenever we are using 3 operands to perform any operation then it is known as Ternary Operator.
points: Also known as Conditional Operator (?:)
Syntax: (condition)?Statement1:Statement2;
If the output of the condition is true then the statement 1 will be executed and when the output of the condition is false then statement 2 will be executed.

flowControl.title: 🌊 Java Flow Control
flowControl.description: Flow control refers to the order in which individual instructions, statements, or function calls are executed or evaluated in a Java program. By default, Java programs execute from top to bottom, but we can change this flow using Flow Control Statements.
points: ✅ Categories of Flow Control Statements
1. Selection Statements (Decision-Making)
2. Iteration Statements (Loops)
3. Transfer Statements (Jump/Branching)

flowControl.title: 1. Selection Statements (Decision Making)
flowControl.description: Used to choose between different paths of execution based on conditions.
points:
a. if-else Statement :-
      _________________________ 

      Syntax:
if (condition) {
    // Executes if condition is true
} else {
    // Executes if condition is false
}
      Rules:
      • Condition must be a boolean expression.
      • Only one block is executed.

      Special Case:,
      • If else is missing, and the condition is false, nothing will be executed.

      b. else-if Ladder :- 
      _____________________________
      Used when we need to check multiple conditions.

      Special Case:,
      • Once a condition evaluates to true, the rest are skipped.,
      • All conditions are checked in top-down order.,

      c. switch Statement :- 
      ____________________________
      Used to replace long else-if ladders for checking multiple fixed values.
      Supported data types (Java 7+):
      • byte, short, char, int, enum, String

      Special/Exceptional Cases:
      • break is optional; if omitted, execution falls through.
      • default is optional but recommended.
      • switch does not support ranges like case 1-3:.

flowControl.title: 2. Iteration Statements (Loops)
flowControl.description: Used to execute a block of code repeatedly.
points:
a. for Loop"
      ________________________________________
      Special Cases:
      • All three parts (init, condition, update) are optional.
      • Multiple variables can be initialized.
      
      b. while Loop
      ________________________________________
      Special Case:
      • If the condition is false initially, loop body won't execute at all.
     
      c. do-while Loop
       ________________________________________
      Special Case:
      • Guarantees at least one execution of loop body.

flowControl.title: 3. Transfer Statements (Jump/Branching)
flowControl.description: Used to change the normal flow of control in loops/switch.
points:
a. break
      ________________________________________
      Used to exit from switch or loop (for, while, do-while).

       Special Case : 
      • Breaks only from the nearest loop or switch.

      b. continue
      ________________________________________
      Skips current iteration and jumps to next cycle.

       Special Case :
      • Does not terminate the loop.

      c. return (Bonus)
      ________________________________________
       Used to exit from a method and return control to the caller.

classes.title: What is a Class?
classes.description: A class is a blueprint for creating objects. It defines properties (variables) and behaviours (methods) that its objects will have. Classes are fundamental to Java's Object-Oriented Programming paradigm.

classes.title: Syntax of a Class
Types of Classes
classes.description: Java provides different types of classes.
points: Concrete Class : A normal class that is fully implemented and can be instantiated.It contains constructors, fields, access modifiers, and can be extended by other classes.
Abstract Class :	A class that cannot be instantiated and serves as a blueprint for subclasses.
It contains both abstract methods (methods without implementation) and concrete methods (methods with implementation).
It Must be extended by subclasses to implement abstract methods.
It provides a common structure for related classes.
Final Class :A class declared using the final keyword and it cannot be extended by any other class.
It ensures class behaviour remains unchanged and protected from modifications
It can contain final methods and final variables
The Final Class cannot be inherited, Final Methods cannot be overridden in subclasses, and Final Variables values cannot be changed after initialization.

classes.title: Inner Classes
classes.description: A class within another class is called a nested class. It helps in better organization and encapsulation.There are four types of nested classes:
points: Static Inner Class : It is defined using the static keyword inside a class and access only static members of the outer class.It does not require an instance of the outer class to be created.
Non-Static Inner Class:A regular class inside another class.Requires an instance of the outer class to be created.
Local Inner Class : Defined inside a method.Accessible only within the method where it is defined.
Anonymous Inner Class : A class without a name.Used for implementing interfaces or extending classes inline.
